name: Jameson Williams
headline: Explosive WR2 whose vertical role finally pays off
body:
Williams turned his draft pedigree into production last season, ranking ninth among receivers with 1,117 yards and seven scores across all 17 games. The efficiency markers are loud: 0.551 EPA per play, a 55.9% success rate, 17.2 yards per catch, and a 34.5% air-yards share that confirms he is Detroit's downfield engine. He ran a route on 95.5% of dropbacks while seeing an 18.5% target share, so the volume is real even if it trails the alpha ahead of him.

The ceiling is obvious, but so is the volatility. Eleven drops and a 10.8% drop rate keep weekly floors shaky, and his deep-leaning usage produces feast-or-famine lines. Detroit broadened his menu with intermediate work, which should stabilize targets, though St. Brown still commands the top look. Treat him as a high-upside WR2 in the mid-rounds, drafting the boom and stomaching the busts.
